actionLibraryList.title: Tabletop Thoracic Rotation
actionLibraryList.context: During the rotation, avoid rushing the movement; maintain slow, controlled motion to prevent overstretching or misalignment.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523622522.jpg","context":"1.Raise one arm with the elbow bent and place your hand behind your head."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749523625921.jpg","context":"1.During the rotation, keep your hand gently touching or lightly supporting your head.\n2.Keep your pelvis and hips stable without moving.\n3.Position your knees hip-width apart."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during the rotation to help relax your body.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: At the maximum range, hold for one to two seconds, feel the stretch in your thoracic spine, and keep your chest open.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: During thoracic spine rotation, keep your hips from rotating.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Front Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the machine handles on the floor mounts.
2.Grab a handle in each hand, keeping your hands close to your chin.
3.Stand with your feet slightly wider than hip-width apart, with your toes pointed slightly outward.
4.Keep your chest up, push your knees forward, and your hips slightly backward.
5.Lower your body with the goal of sitting between your feet.
6.Lower until your quads are slightly below horizontal.
7.Drive through your heels to lift your body back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the handles to floor level.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your spine in a neutral position and the handles near your chin.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Engage your core to stabilize your body.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Take a deep breath at the beginning of the movement, engage your core, and slowly exhale during the ascent.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Flex and squeeze the quads during the squat, incorporating the energy from your knees.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Maintain a neutral spine alignment.
2.Grab a handle in each hand, keeping your hands close to your chin.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750231548845.jpg"},{"context":"1.Lower into a squat, keeping your back upright until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
2.Ensure your knees are in line with your toes, avoiding any inward movement of the knees.
3.Slightly rotate your feet outward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750231552600.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Take a deep breath at the beginning of the movement, engage your core, and slowly exhale during the ascent.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Flex and squeeze the quads during the squat, incorporating the energy from your knees.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Split Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Grab a handle in each hand and set up in the lunge stance.
3.Keep your chest up and lower your rear knee toward the floor.
4.Ensure your front knee stays over your foot (don't let it drift sideways).
5.Aim for a 90-degree angle in both knees.
6.Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the handles to the floor position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Inhale as you squat, exhale as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Stand up by contracting your quads and glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Stand tall with your back upright.
2.Set up a staggered stance.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751508735550.jpg"},{"context":"1.Stand tall with your core tight.
2.Align your toes and knees as you squat.
3.Avoid touching the floor with your back knee, and engage your glutes throughout.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751508742566.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Keep your breath steady. Inhale as you lower yourself into the squat, and exhale as you lift back up.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Rise to the starting position by contracting your quads and glutes.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Tricep Rope Upright Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Keep your wrists stable and pull the rope straight up in front of your body.
2.Keep elbows slightly above your forearms throughout the lift.
3.Pause briefly at the top, then slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the cable to bottom position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Grab the ends of the tricep rope with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Your arms should be fully extended in front of you, with a slight bend in your elbows.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Begin the movement by pulling the rope upward towards your chin.Keep the rope close to your body, and lead with your elbows rather than your hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Avoid using your lower back to lift the weight. Keep your torso upright and core engaged. Avoid shrugging shoulder.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748143449168.jpg","context":"1.Stand shoulder width.
2.Keep core tight.
3.Relax arm and grab tricep rope."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748143453514.jpg","context":"1.Keep feet parallel or slightly turned out.
2.Keep core tight.
3.Lift the rope to chest height."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Do not use your lower back to lift; keep your shoulders relaxed and down.

actionLibraryList.title: Sprinter Start
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start in a kneeling lunge position with the back knee slightly off the floor.
2.Explosively drive the rear knee forward and up to initiate the jump.
3.Land softly and return the leg to the starting position.
4.Use your arms to stabilize and generate more power.
5.Keep your chest up and eyes forward throughout the movement.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Start with a lunge, lean forward. touch the floor with hand.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Explosively drive rear knee forward and up to initiate a jump.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Keep your core nice and tight.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Reach your hand toward the ground.\n2.Start in a kneeling lunge position.\n3.Keep your back knee slightly off the floor.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750736552057.jpg"},{"context":"1.Jump and drive your knee up fast.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750736591716.jpg"}]

actionLibraryList.title: Supine Bench Tricep Rope Crunch
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your core engaged and your lower back pressed against the bench. Focus on using your abdominal muscles to lift your upper body off the bench.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749204061727.jpg","context":"1.Lie down on the bench.
2.Keep your feet flat on the floor with your knees bent.
3.Bend your elbows and grasp the handles with both hands."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749204065289.jpg","context":"1.Keep your core engaged and your lower back pressed against the bench.
2.Focus on activating your abdominal muscles to lift your upper body off the bench."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale during exertion, inhale during the return phase.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid pulling with your arms, and keep your lower back pressed against the bench.
